content: Hi, I'm Devon Clark. I've been working as an AI ethics researcher, and I'm dealing with chronic back pain. I need to find some new tools to help me manage my work and health.
Hi Devon, I'm so sorry to hear about your back pain. What kind of tools are you looking for to help manage your work and health?
I've been using a standing desk, but I'm not sure it's enough. I also love urban sketching in my free time, and I wish there was an app that could help me track my progress and find new inspiration.
There are several great apps for urban sketching, Devon. You might enjoy Sketchbook Pro or Autodesk Sketchbook. For back pain management, I can suggest some apps like StretchIt or Back Pain Manager.
Those sound great, thanks! I'll look into them. I've also been trying to find a task management tool that can help me prioritize my work and deadlines.
I'd recommend checking out tools like Trello, Asana, or Todoist, Devon. They can help you organize your tasks, set reminders, and even collaborate with colleagues.
Awesome, thanks! I'm really looking forward to trying those out. By the way, I'm planning to run a marathon on the 1st of May, and I'm trying to get my training back on track despite the back pain.

That's a challenging but admirable goal, Devon! Make sure to listen to your body and take regular breaks during training. Have you considered using a running app like Nike Run Club or Strava to track your progress and stay motivated?
That's a great idea, thanks! I've been hiking with my friends in the mountains to help with my training, and it's been really helpful.
Hiking is a great way to cross-train, Devon. The varied terrain and scenery can help keep your workouts interesting and prevent boredom.
I'm glad I started doing it. I've also been looking into treatment options for my back pain, and I'm considering physical therapy or chiropractic care.

Both physical therapy and chiropractic care can be effective for managing back pain, Devon. I'd recommend consulting with a healthcare professional to determine the best course of treatment for your specific needs.
Thanks for the advice. I think I'll schedule a consultation soon.
You're welcome, Devon. Remember to prioritize your health and take care of your back during your marathon training.
I will, thanks. I'm looking forward to trying out these new tools and getting my training back on track.
